autism_relevance: paroxetine prescribed for non-autism comorbid indication; PGx caution per CPIC
notes: Bousman 2023 CPIC SSRI/SNRI guideline. CYP2D6 PM → consider alternative drug or 50% dose reduction.

autism_relevance: same caveat as PGX-0001
notes: Bousman 2023 CPIC. CYP2D6 UM → alternative drug recommended; reduced efficacy expected.

autism_relevance: same caveat
notes: Bousman 2023 CPIC. Fluoxetine has long half-life and active metabolites; PM cumulative effects.

autism_relevance: citalopram for non-autism indication; CYP2C19 PM raises QT-prolongation risk
notes: Bousman 2023 CPIC. CYP2C19 PM + citalopram → 50% dose reduction; FDA black-box for QT.

autism_relevance: same caveat
notes: Bousman 2023 CPIC. Escitalopram PM → 50% dose reduction or alternative.

autism_relevance: same caveat
notes: Bousman 2023 CPIC. CYP2C19 UM → alternative; reduced efficacy.

autism_relevance: same caveat
notes: Bousman 2023 CPIC. Sertraline CYP2C19 PM → 50% dose reduction.

autism_relevance: TCAs sometimes used for sleep, mood, neuropathic pain in autistic individuals; PGx safety
notes: Hicks 2017 CPIC TCA. CYP2D6 PM → alternative or 50% dose reduce. Cardiotoxicity risk.

autism_relevance: same caveat
notes: Hicks 2017 CPIC TCA. CYP2D6 UM → subtherapeutic levels; alternative.

autism_relevance: clomipramine sometimes used for severe OCD in autistic individuals
notes: Hicks 2017 CPIC TCA. CYP2C19 PM → 50% dose reduce.

autism_relevance: post-surgical pain control; UM converts codeine→morphine rapidly causing respiratory depression
notes: Crews 2021 CPIC opioid. CYP2D6 UM + codeine = AVOID. FDA black-box especially in children. Major safety concern.

autism_relevance: same caveat
notes: Crews 2021 CPIC. CYP2D6 PM + codeine = no analgesic effect; alternative needed.

autism_relevance: post-surgical
notes: Crews 2021 CPIC. Tramadol like codeine — UM → respiratory depression risk.

autism_relevance: opioid response / addiction risk modifier
notes: Crews 2021 CPIC. OPRM1 A118G G allele → reduced opioid response per some studies; CPIC level-of-evidence moderate.

autism_relevance: COMT V/V → increased pain sensitivity / reduced opioid response
notes: Crews 2021 CPIC. COMT V/V (high enzyme activity) → reduced central pain modulation.

autism_relevance: statins occasionally used in autism comorbid hypercholesterolemia; SLCO1B1 myopathy risk
notes: Cooper-DeHoff 2022 CPIC. SLCO1B1 *5/*5 + simvastatin → high myopathy risk; alternative recommended.

autism_relevance: carbamazepine for autism comorbid epilepsy / mood; HLA-B*15:02 raises Stevens-Johnson syndrome risk substantially in Asian ancestry populations
notes: Phillips 2018 CPIC HLA. HLA-B*15:02 + carbamazepine = AVOID; SJS/TEN risk especially Asian populations.

autism_relevance: phenytoin for autism comorbid epilepsy
notes: Phillips 2018 CPIC. HLA-B*15:02 + phenytoin = AVOID. SJS/TEN risk.

autism_relevance: oxcarbazepine cross-reactivity risk for SJS in HLA-B*15:02
notes: Phillips 2018 CPIC. HLA-B*15:02 + oxcarbazepine = elevated SJS risk; alternative.

variant_definition_summary: MTHFR C677T T/T (~30% reduced enzyme activity)
autism_relevance: MTHFR T/T cannot fully reduce folic acid; methylfolate (5-MTHF) bypasses
notes: James 2004 Am J Clin Nutr — methylation cycle in autism. Mechanistic basis (not formal CPIC) for using 5-MTHF over folic acid in MTHFR-impaired children. Confidence MODERATE.

variant_definition_summary: COMT V158M M/M (low enzyme activity, overmethylator-leaning)
autism_relevance: COMT M/M overmethylators may experience paradoxical agitation/anxiety with high-dose methylfolate
notes: James 2004 framework. COMT M/M slow methyl-clearance + high methylfolate = methyl-trap symptoms (agitation, anxiety, insomnia). Walsh framework calls these "overmethylators." Confidence MODERATE; mechanistic + clinical observation, not RCT.

autism_relevance: same as PGX-0021 — COMT M/M caution with methyl-B12
notes: Same Walsh-framework mechanistic basis as methylfolate. Hydroxocobalamin is alternative B12 form for COMT M/M.

variant_definition_summary: MTRR A66G A/A (full activity; G/G slows methionine cycle regen)
autism_relevance: MTRR variants modulate B12-dependent methionine cycle regeneration
notes: James 2004. MTRR G/G reduces methionine synthase reactivation, increases B12 demand for methylation cycle.

variant_definition_summary: DPYD *2A or other null allele homozygous
autism_relevance: rare; for autism + comorbid cancer treatment context
notes: Amstutz 2018 CPIC DPYD. DPYD PM + 5-FU = severe/lethal toxicity; AVOID.

autism_relevance: thiopurines for autism comorbid IBD or autoimmune disease
notes: Relling 2019 CPIC. TPMT PM + azathioprine = severe myelosuppression; 90% dose reduce or alternative.

autism_relevance: rare autism context; for adult autism + cardiovascular
notes: Lee 2022 CPIC. CYP2C19 PM + clopidogrel = no antiplatelet effect; use alternative.

autism_relevance: rare context
notes: Johnson 2017 CPIC. CYP2C9 PM + warfarin = bleeding risk; dose adjust or DOAC.

autism_relevance: rare — for autism + organ transplant comorbidity
notes: Birdwell 2015 CPIC. CYP3A5*1 expressers need ~1.5x standard tacrolimus dose for therapeutic levels.

autism_relevance: rare context
notes: Martin 2014 CPIC. HLA-B*57:01 + abacavir = severe hypersensitivity; contraindicated. Pre-prescription HLA-B*57:01 screening is standard of care.

autism_relevance: framework
notes: Swen 2011 DPWG — Dutch Pharmacogenetics Working Group guidelines. Reference row for the alternative-to-CPIC framework. Engine prefers CPIC where both exist; DPWG used for drugs not yet covered by CPIC.

autism_relevance: atomoxetine commonly prescribed for ADHD comorbid with autism; CYP2D6 PM 10-fold higher exposure
notes: [H-9 audit fix] Sauer 2005 Clin Pharmacokinet (PMID 15910008) — atomoxetine clinical pharmacokinetics review. CYP2D6 PMs have ~10x higher AUC vs extensive metabolizers; FDA labeling notes higher AE rates. Reduce dose or use with caution per CPIC-supplementary guidance (no formal CPIC atomoxetine guideline at time of writing).

autism_relevance: same
notes: [H-9 audit fix] Sauer 2005. CYP2D6 UMs may need higher doses or experience reduced efficacy.

autism_relevance: methylphenidate commonly prescribed for ADHD in autism comorbidity; CES1 hydrolyzes methylphenidate
notes: [H-9 audit fix] Brown 2022 Front Pediatr (PMID 36741090) — CES1 variant rs71647871 G143E associates with elevated methylphenidate plasma levels and increased AE in pediatric ADHD. Carboxylesterase-1 hydrolyzes methylphenidate; decreased function = higher exposure. PGx testing emerging in pediatric ADHD.

autism_relevance: risperidone FDA-approved for autism irritability; CYP2D6 PM raises EPS, prolactin, sedation risk
notes: [H-9 audit fix] de Leon 2020 Pharmacogenomics (PMID 33054667) — 25-year reflection on CYP2D6 + risperidone. CYP2D6 PMs accumulate parent risperidone (active); EPS, hyperprolactinemia, weight gain risk increased. Dose reduction or alternative antipsychotic appropriate.

autism_relevance: same as PGX-0034
notes: [H-9 audit fix] Zhang 2020 Pharmacotherapy (PMID 32519344) — systematic review of CYP2D6 + risperidone PK. UMs have lower steady-state risperidone; subtherapeutic risk.

autism_relevance: aripiprazole FDA-approved for autism irritability; CYP2D6 PM dose-reduction explicitly in FDA label
notes: [H-9 audit fix] FDA aripiprazole label specifies CYP2D6 PM dose reduction. de Leon 2020 (PMID 33054667) reviews antipsychotic-CYP2D6 PGx including aripiprazole. Aripiprazole is largely CYP2D6 + CYP3A4 metabolized; PM accumulates parent drug.

autism_relevance: amphetamine prescribed for ADHD comorbid with autism
notes: [H-9 audit fix] Schmid 2016 Pharmacogenet Genomics (PMID 27253829) — CYP2D6 function moderates MDMA pharmacokinetics; mechanism extends to amphetamine class via CYP2D6-mediated p-hydroxylation. Effect smaller than for methylphenidate (CES1 dominant) or atomoxetine (CYP2D6 dominant). Monitor for AE.
